content: We can reframe the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a meta-supervisor for bootstrapping multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where each iteration of Self-Refine pulls from a different inconsistent solving perspective to generate self-feedback, rather than refining a single output from a fixed view. This creates a hierarchical reflection system that retains Self-Refine’s key advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data while addressing the limitation of single-perspective self-feedback that plagues many basic reflection methods.
